moveAnnotations.annotation: We're playing the Grob Opening, an unusual and provocative first move that immediately challenges Black's central control on the flanks rather than fighting for the center directly. By advancing the g-pawn, we control the f4 and h3 squares, potentially preparing a fianchetto setup on g2 while keeping Black guessing about our intentions. This move is rarely seen at the highest levels but has practical value: it avoids heavily-analyzed mainlines and puts the onus on Black to prove their counterplay, while we maintain flexibility to pursue either a kingside fianchetto or even a central advance depending on Black's response. Be warned though—this is a fundamentally offbeat choice, so you'll need good intuition rather than memorized theory.
